design_goal: Make vishing a first-class channel in the Red Team Campaign Launcher by inheriting the existing wizard and reporting infrastructure and replacing only the steps where voice is genuinely different from text.
constraints: Vishing-only campaigns in v1 — no mixing with SMS or WhatsApp
Admin requests campaign; Dune operators activate delivery
Voice AEPs non-interchangeable with text AEPs
Voice AEPs are structurally richer: Persona + Scenario as two separately-versioned components
Facts/OSINT stored per-organization and injected into the AI at runtime; admin reviews before campaign submission
Remediation suppressed by default; locked at launch
Caller ID spoofing capability not confirmed
IDP SCIM required for per-target timezone enforcement
Outlined badge variant may not exist in DS v2 — requires confirmation
selected_strategy: Channel-Swapped Wizard with Five Targeted Enhancements
selected_strategy_rationale: Inherits the 8-step wizard and reporting infrastructure completely. Applies five focused enhancements at trust-critical departure points: (1) split Step 4 compliance pre-flight (verified vs. self-certified), (2) Pending Activation trust layer, (3) Call Log badge differentiation (outlined vs. filled gray), (4) Voice AEP builder Channel Type consequence preview (now showing 3-step structure), (5) OSINT review as a named campaign step (Step 3.5) making AI realism transparent and admin-accountable.

alternative_strategies.name: Pure Inheritance
alternative_strategies.summary: Treat vishing as a third channel with minimal new patterns.
alternative_strategies.rejected_because: Compliance self-certifications would look like platform-verified states. Two gray badges are visually indistinguishable at scale. OSINT injection would be invisible to admins — significant trust and accountability gap.

alternative_strategies.name: Full Redesign
alternative_strategies.summary: Treat vishing as so different from text that every step gets a bespoke design.
alternative_strategies.rejected_because: Abandons wizard infrastructure that gives the product its consistency. Creates unnecessary design debt and slows engineering.

voice_aep_builder_steps: Step 1 — Persona: caller name, claimed role, greeting message, voice selector, tone, cadence, background audio, banned phrases
Step 2 — Scenario: phases, collectibles, tactics, goals + live call flow visualization (diagram updates in real time)
Step 3 — Test & Refine: AI refine panel, Scenario Flow tab, Live Test tab (call + post-call phase/collectible highlights)

elements: Phase nodes (name, goal, collectible dots, tactic chips)
Goal summary card at terminal node
scenario_visualization_design.interaction: Read-only (editing happens in left panel form only)
scenario_visualization_design.test_call_behavior: After each test call, Scenario Flow tab overlays which phases were reached and which collectibles fired
scenario_visualization_design.export: PNG download for stakeholder presentations or debrief documents

osint_review_step.wizard_position: Step 3.5 — between Voice AEP selection and Compliance Pre-flight
osint_review_step.title: Review Organizational Intelligence
osint_review_step.scenario_relevance_column: Shows phase badges matching the selected Voice AEP's scenario phases — tells admin exactly when each fact will be used
osint_review_step.empty_state: Guidance to contact Dune representative; admin can proceed with warning
osint_review_step.continue_condition: Always available; contextual warning if fewer than 3 facts active

tradeoffs: Group B acknowledgment checklist is a new DS pattern requiring DS review before implementation
Outlined badge variant requires DS confirmation — new variant needed if it doesn't exist
Manual VOIP event fallback for test call allows self-attestation — pragmatic but removes certainty that admin reviewed persona live
Scenario visualization is a novel rendering surface — may require a new DS graph primitive or custom SVG/canvas component
Step 3.5 Scenario relevance column is AEP-dependent — changes if admin goes back and changes the selected AEP
assumptions: The 8-step wizard pattern from the Red Team Campaign Launcher is the correct base
Susceptibility rate denominator decision (Reached vs. Total Targets) will be made by PM
Campaign-specific calling notes have a 500-char limit to prevent ops panel overflow
Zero Reached stat shows 'N/A' for susceptibility rate, not '0%'
open_issues: Is Group B compliance acknowledgment a self-certification or document upload flow?
Does outlined badge variant exist in DS v2?
What is Dune's committed activation SLA?
Is debrief out-of-platform, and should the Overview tab include debrief resources?
Does the VOIP AI system emit semantic outcome events automatically, or does ops classify post-call?
How is the facts database populated for new customers?
Is fact suppression per-campaign or persistent?
Can the DS diagram component support the scenario visualization, or is a custom component needed for v1?
risks: Scenario visualization is novel and may require significant Eng investment if a DS graph primitive doesn't exist
OSINT review step requires facts to be populated — empty state for new customers degrades the step significantly
Group B acknowledgment checklist scope may be larger if document upload is required
Caller ID spoofing limitation not surfaced in UI — admins may expect wrong behavior
Pending Activation SLA commitment not confirmed — '1 business day' copy may create support liability
ds_patterns_used: wizard — 8-step with persistent step bar (inherited from Red Team Campaign Launcher)
status card — Group A platform-verified compliance checks (inherited from text campaign Step 4)
badge — outcome states in Call Log table (extended with outlined variant)
drawer — call transcript / recording detail (inherited from text campaign Conversations tab)
table — Call Log table; Facts review table (Step 3.5)
new_patterns_required: Acknowledgment checklist (Group B, Step 4) — multi-item vertical checklist for admin self-certifications
Outlined badge variant — 'Not Yet Called' state requires hollow/outlined visual treatment
Scenario visualization diagram — phase nodes, transition arrows, tactic chips, collectibles legend. Novel component. DS review required.
Facts review table with scenario relevance column (Step 3.5) — phase badge cells are AEP-context-aware
next_actions: Confirm DS v2 badge variants — check whether outlined badge exists
Flag Group B acknowledgment checklist for DS review
Define scenario visualization component spec and confirm rendering approach with Eng (SVG, canvas, or DS graph primitive)
Begin design with AEP builder Persona step — establish voice-specific field vocabulary
Design Scenario step with visualization panel — highest novelty element in the feature
Design Step 3.5 OSINT Review — facts table, category structure, empty state, suppression state
Use existing Red Team Campaign Launcher Figma file as base — copy step frames for Steps 1, 2, 6, 8; create new frames for Steps 3, 3.5, 4, 5, 7
